expressions.english: clean water
clean sea
clean environment
clean window
clean country
clean air
clean skin; clear skin
clean bathroom; clean toilet
The house has become really clean.
Your skin seems very clear.
expressions.notes: - 것 같다 = It seems/looks like + S + V

expressions.english: The street here is very clean.
I'll go wash the cup clean.
expressions.notes:
컵 = cup
씻다 = to wash
-이/어 올다 = to go + V + and come back

expressions.english: Just by tidying things up well, it (the place) looks clean.
expressions.notes:
정리정돈하다 = to tidy things up
-이/어 보이다 = to look + adjective

expressions.english: It's good to work in a clean office.
I have wiped the windows clean.

expressions.korean: 손을 깨끗하게 씻어야 감기에 안 걸려요.
expressions.english: You should wash your hands so that you won't catch a cold.
expressions.notes:
손 = hand
-이/어야 해야 = should + V1 + in order to + V2
감기 = cold, flu
감기에 걸리다 = to catch a cold

expressions.korean: 유리창이 너무 깨끗해서 없는 줄 알았어요.
expressions.english: The window was really clean, so I thought it wasn't there.
expressions.notes: -(으)ㄴ/는 줄 알다 = to mistakenly think + S + V

expressions.korean: 책상이 깨끗해야 일도 더 잘 되는 것 같아요.
expressions.english: I feel like I can work better when my desk is clean.
expressions.notes:
책상 = desk
더 = more
잘 되다 = to become successful; to become fine

expressions.korean: 깨끗한 공기를 마시니까 기분이 정말 좋아요.
expressions.english: Getting some fresh air makes me feel really good.
expressions.notes:
마시다 = to drink
기분 = feelings; mood
정말 = really; very

expressions.korean: 오늘 손님이 오니까 사무실을 깨끗하게 청소해요.
expressions.english: A guest is coming today, so let's clean up the office.
expressions.notes:
손님 = guest, visitor
오다 = to come
청소하다 = to clean up
